fullDescription.text: The application constructs SQL statements from user-controlled input without proper parameterization, enabling an attacker to alter query logic or access unauthorized data.
User-controlled input is rendered in the browser without output encoding, allowing injection of arbitrary JavaScript that executes in victim browser context.
The application contains hard-coded credentials such as passwords, API keys, or cryptographic keys embedded in source code.
The application deserializes data from untrusted sources without validation, enabling remote code execution via crafted serialized objects.

shortDescription.text: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

fullDescription.text: User-supplied filename or path is used to access files on the server without proper sanitization, allowing directory traversal to read or write arbitrary files.
The application accepts a URL or similar input from the user and retrieves the resource at that URL without restricting the target, enabling requests to internal services.

shortDescription.text: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

fullDescription.text: The application uses MD5, SHA1, or other deprecated hash algorithms for security-sensitive operations such as password hashing or integrity verification.

shortDescription.text: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

fullDescription.text: The XML parser processes external entity references in untrusted XML input, potentially enabling file disclosure, SSRF, or denial of service.

shortDescription.text: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

fullDescription.text: Error handlers or debug endpoints expose stack traces, database schema details, internal IP addresses, or other sensitive information to end users.
State-changing endpoints lack CSRF token validation, allowing attackers to trick authenticated users into performing unintended actions.
The application does not enforce authorization checks before granting access to a resource or performing a privileged operation.

shortDescription.text: Improper Control of Generation of Code

fullDescription.text: User input is passed to a code evaluation function such as eval(), Runtime.exec(), or template engines without sanitization, enabling arbitrary code execution.

message.text: User search query concatenated directly into SQL statement via String.format() instead of PreparedStatement. Attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the 'q' parameter to exfiltrate user records including email addresses and hashed passwords.

description.text: Replace String.format SQL with parameterized query: entityManager.createQuery("SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.name LIKE :query").setParameter("query", "%" + query + "%")

insertedContent.text: entityManager.createQuery("SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.name LIKE :query", User.class).setParameter("query", "%" + query + "%").getResultList()

message.text: Product name rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization. Stored XSS possible if product name contains malicious script injected via admin API or third-party product feed.

description.text:
Replace dangerouslySetInnerHTML with DOMPurify.sanitize() or use React's default text rendering:

{product.name}

message.text: Stripe API secret key hardcoded in application.properties. Key pattern 'sk_live_...' detected. This key provides full access to the Stripe account including charge creation and refund processing.

description.text: Move to environment variable or AWS Secrets Manager: stripe.api.key=${STRIPE_API_KEY}

message.text: ObjectInputStream.readObject() called on untrusted data from order queue message body without ObjectInputFilter. Attacker can craft serialized Java object to achieve remote code execution via commons-collections gadget chain.

description.text: Replace ObjectInputStream with JSON deserialization (Jackson ObjectMapper) or add JEP 290 ObjectInputFilter to allowlist only Order.class

message.text: User-supplied filename passed directly to new File(uploadDir, filename) without path traversal sanitization. Attacker can upload file with name '../../../etc/cron.d/backdoor' to write arbitrary files on the server.

description.text: Sanitize filename: Path resolved = uploadPath.resolve(filename).normalize(); if (!resolved.startsWith(uploadPath)) throw new SecurityException("Path traversal");

message.text: Webhook callback URL from request body passed to HttpClient without URL scheme or hostname validation. Attacker can set callback_url to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ to access EC2 instance metadata and steal AWS IAM role credentials.

description.text: Validate URL against allowlist of permitted domains. Block RFC1918 ranges, link-local (169.254.x.x), and localhost. Use IMDSv2 on EC2 instances.

message.text: MessageDigest.getInstance("MD5") used for password reset token generation. MD5 has known collision attacks and 2^18 complexity for preimage. Use SHA-256 or bcrypt for security-sensitive hashing.

description.text: Replace MD5 with SecureRandom for token generation: byte[] token = new byte[32]; new SecureRandom().nextBytes(token); return Base64.getUrlEncoder().encodeToString(token);

message.text: DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance() used without disabling external entity processing. XML product feed parser allows XXE injection to read local files or perform SSRF via DOCTYPE declarations in supplier XML feeds.

Global exception handler returns full stack trace including class names, line numbers, and SQL query fragments in HTTP 500 responses. Exposes Hibernate version, table schema, and internal service topology.

description.text: Return generic error message to client. Log full stack trace server-side with correlation ID. Return only {"error": "Internal server error", "correlationId": uuid} to client.

message.text: PaymentController.processPayment() POST endpoint accepts requests without CSRF token validation. Spring Security CSRF is disabled globally via http.csrf().disable(). Attacker can craft a malicious page that auto-submits a payment form when visited by an authenticated user.

description.text: Re-enable CSRF protection: Remove http.csrf().disable(). Use CookieCsrfTokenRepository.withHttpOnlyFalse() for SPA compatibility. Add X-XSRF-TOKEN header to frontend requests.

message.text: AdminAPI.deleteUser() endpoint has no @PreAuthorize or role check. Any authenticated user (including customers) can call DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} to delete arbitrary user accounts including other administrators.

description.text: Add @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')") annotation. Verify caller identity matches admin role in SecurityContext. Log all admin actions to audit trail.

message.text: Thymeleaf template engine processes user-supplied template fragments via __${expression}__ preprocessor syntax. Attacker can inject SpEL expressions to execute arbitrary Java code on the server including Runtime.exec() for OS command injection.

description.text: Never pass user input to Thymeleaf preprocessor expressions. Use th:text for rendering user content. Disable SpEL evaluation in template fragments. Move to restricted evaluation context.
